title: leonurus glabra
intro: Leonurus glabra, also known as Chinese motherwort or small-leaved motherwort, is a perennial herb in the mint family with a long history of medicinal use. Traditionally, it has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and palpitations, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system and promote cardiovascular health. Leonurus glabra can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
